title: Create and monitor a study
description: Configure a study, watch the trials fill in live, and read the terminal state — the core Karpathy loop.

screenshots.caption: Open the Studies page. Every study you create lands here with its name, cluster, status, best-so-far metric, and timestamps. The newly seeded `tune-product-title-boost-baseline-{suffix}` study sits at the top while earlier completed studies (`tune-jobtitle-vs-company-boost`, `add-7day-freshness-decay-news`, `reduce-fuzziness-helpcenter-search`) anchor the rest of the list. The filter chips along the top scope by lifecycle stage.

Click 'queued' to filter the list to studies waiting for the orchestrator. The URL updates to `?status=queued` so the filter is sharable + bookmarkable. The empty state here is an expected timing artifact — every seeded study completed before this screenshot was taken (max_trials=2 ran in under a second). Studies move queued → running → (completed | failed | cancelled) as the orchestrator processes them.

Click 'Create study' to open the multi-step modal. Step 1 binds the study to a cluster + target index. The Target field is now a dropdown — it shows the disabled 'Pick a cluster first' placeholder until you pick a cluster, then populates from that cluster's index list (scoped by the cluster's `target_filter` glob, e.g. `products*` for an e-commerce cluster). The 'Enter manually' toggle below the field is always visible and falls back to a free-text input — useful when the operator-supplied glob is restrictive or the cluster returns 403 on index listing. Subsequent wizard steps pick the query set + judgment list, the query template, the search-space (which params Optuna will vary + their ranges), and the objective metric + budget.

The detail page is where the operator spends real time. Header surfaces the study name, status badge, target index (`products`), best-so-far metric, and trial summary. The trials table below populates in real-time as Optuna's TPE sampler proposes `title_boost` values and the worker scores them against the imported judgments via `ir_measures`. The page auto-polls every 3 seconds while the study is running.

Terminal state. Once `max_trials` is reached (or the operator cancels via the 'Cancel study' button, which fires `POST /api/v1/studies/{id}/cancel`), the status moves to a terminal value and the Cancel button is grayed out — this study is `completed` so cancel is no longer actionable. Cancellation is graceful — in-flight trials complete cleanly; no new trials enqueue. Once terminal, the orchestrator emits a digest + (when judgments meet the quality threshold) a pending proposal that you'll review on the Proposals page.
